name: 06_Sentiment_Analysis_SVM_Sentiment140_ML
query: Por favor, ayúdame a construir un sistema para el análisis de sentimientos en tweets utilizando el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 disponible en Hugging Face. Carga el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 y, al cargar los datos, límpialos eliminando palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`. Usa Word2Vec o GloVe para la vectorización de texto. Esto debe ocurrir en `src/data_loader.py`. Luego, implementa y entrena un clasificador SVM en `src/model.py`. Finalmente, escribe un informe de la precisión del clasificador en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`. Idealmente, el informe debe ser fácilmente interpretable.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.
El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.
Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.
La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

preferences.criteria: El proceso de descarga del conjunto de datos debe ser confiable, con un manejo claro de errores.
El informe final de precisión debe ser directo y fácil de interpretar.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement specifies that the "Sentiment140" dataset should be obtained in `src/data_loader.py`. However, the provided content of `data_loader.py` does not include any actual implementation for downloading or loading the dataset from Hugging Face. The function `load_dataset()` is merely a placeholder and does not contain code to access or retrieve the dataset. Although the trajectory evidence indicates that the dataset was eventually loaded successfully, the code provided does not demonstrate this functionality.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The provided content of `src/data_loader.py` does not include any implementation details for data cleaning, such as removing stop words, punctuation, or special characters. The file contains placeholders and minimal semantic implementation, but no actual code performing the required data cleaning operations. Additionally, while the trajectory evidence mentions a syntax error related to data cleaning that was corrected, there is no specific evidence or code provided that demonstrates the implementation of the cleaning process.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement specifies that word embeddings, either "Word2Vec" or "GloVe", should be used to convert text to vectors in `src/data_loader.py`. While the trajectory evidence indicates an attempt to load "Word2Vec" and a successful execution using "GloVe", the provided content of `data_loader.py` does not show any actual implementation of text conversion to vectors using these embeddings. The file contains only placeholders and does not demonstrate the required functionality.

judge_stats.criteria: Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement specifies that an "SVM classifier" should be implemented and trained in `src/model.py`. However, the content of `model.py` provided does not show any implementation of an SVM classifier. Instead, it contains a placeholder function `build_model()` that does not relate to SVM. Although the trajectory evidence mentions that an SVM classifier was trained successfully after resolving import issues, the actual implementation details are missing from the provided code content.

judge_stats.criteria: La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement is not fully met because, although the accuracy score is printed correctly in the console, the file `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt` contains a placeholder and not the actual accuracy score. This is due to a write error caused by insufficient permissions in the destination directory, as indicated in the trajectory evidence.
